exec: var r = pm.response.json(); if (r.token) pm.collectionVariables.set('token', r.token);
var r = pm.response.json(); if (r.token) pm.collectionVariables.set('token', r.token);
var r = pm.response.json(); if (r.token) pm.collectionVariables.set('web_token', r.token);
var r = pm.response.json(); if (r.token) pm.collectionVariables.set('guest_token', r.token);

query.description: Filter by room type (e.g. standard, deluxe, suite)

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Room 101",
  "room_type": "standard",
  "capacity": 2,
  "price_per_night": 350.00,
  "floor": "1",
  "description": "Comfortable standard room with garden view",
  "amenities": ["WiFi", "Air Conditioning", "TV", "En-suite Bathroom"]
}

request.description: venue_type: conference_hall | event_space | boardroom | outdoor | dining. rate_type: hourly | daily. base_rate is read here (not from booking payloads) and is what an event booking is priced from (base_rate × days).

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Main Conference Hall",
  "venue_type": "conference_hall",
  "capacity": 200,
  "base_rate": 8000,
  "rate_type": "daily",
  "floor": "Ground Floor",
  "area_sqm": 250.5,
  "notes": "Built-in projector and PA system",
  "amenities": ["Projector", "PA System", "Air Conditioning", "Wi-Fi"],
  "images": []
}

request.description: Named guests, each picking menu items. Every selection is a menu item with a quantity — the price is looked up server-side from menu_items (no price field is sent). Replace menu_item_id values with real ids from POST /api/v1/menu/items.
No named roster — a headcount plus a top-level buffet item. The buffet is itself a menu item; quantity drives billing (here one cover per head). pax = headcount; no attendees are created.

Conference / gala / wedding etc. are all 'event' — event_type names the kind. The guest picks a venue at submission (venue_id, required); approval auto-creates the booking priced at venue.base_rate × days. Roster is optional: omit guests and rely on headcount, or name some/all.

For meals requests the response includes a meals_summary object: each payload menu_item_id resolved to its current name + unit_price, with per-line, per-guest and estimated_total (pre-VAT) — this is what the back-office workflow task renders.

Event and meals requests auto-materialise into a booking on approval (event → venue reservation; meals → orders), plus a draft invoice. Accommodation only marks the request approved — staff then assign rooms via Materialise to Booking.
Accommodation only — assign a room to each guest (guest_index maps to the position in the request's guests array). Event and meals materialise automatically on approve and do not use this endpoint.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Grilled Tilapia",

  "price": 85.00,
  "category": "main",
  "is_available": true
}
